name: Fayón - Maella - Caspe — circular from Mequinenza
name_original: Fayón - Maella - Caspe — circular desde Mequinenza
region_intro: Road cycling around Pantà de Riba-roja offers diverse routes across varied terrain, encompassing the Ebro River basin. The region is characterized by the expansive reservoir, which stretches across several municipalities, and the unique Aiguabarreig confluence of the Segre, Cinca, and Ebro rivers. Cyclists can navigate routes that hug the riverbanks, providing views of the natural environment and surrounding landscapes. The area presents options ranging from routes with minimal elevation gain to more challenging rides with significant ascents. Best road cycling routes around Pantà de Riba-roja The most popular road cycling route is Fayón - Maella - Caspe — circular from Mequinenza, a 77.4 miles (124.5 km) trail that takes about 6 hours 7 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Riba-roja d'Ebre – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Faió - la Pobla de Massaluca, a difficult 44.6 miles (71.8 km) path. This route offers views of the Riba-roja Reservoir and surrounding landscapes, with substantial climbing. Local road cyclists also love the Cinca River – Fraga loop from Mequinenza / Mequinensa, a 25.5 miles (41.0 km) trail leading through the Cinca River area, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Road cycling around Pantà de Riba-roja is defined by the Ebro River, the expansive reservoir, and varied terrain with both flat sections and significant hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Pantà de Riba-roja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pantà de Riba-roja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Pantà de Riba-roja?
A: The region offers diverse terrain, from routes that hug the Ebro River banks with minimal elevation gain to more challenging rides with significant ascents. You'll find a varied landscape, including both flat sections and substantial hills, characteristic of the Ebro basin.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Pantà de Riba-roja?
A: Yes, Pantà de Riba-roja has routes suitable for different fitness levels. Out of the 29 available road cycling routes, 4 are classified as easy. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cinca River – Fraga loop from Mequinenza / Mequinensa&lt;/a&gt;, which is 25.5 miles (41.0 km) long and takes about 1 hour 48 minutes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in the area?
A: The area is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can cycle along the Ebro River and the expansive reservoir. A key natural highlight is the Aiguabarreig, where the Segre, Cinca, and Ebro rivers converge. Historical points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6444930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Church Tower of Fayón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4644675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Mequinenza&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. The region features numerous difficult routes with significant elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992605850/fayon-maella-caspe-circular-from-mequinenza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fayón - Maella - Caspe — circular from Mequinenza&lt;/a&gt; route is 77.4 miles (124.5 km) with substantial climbing, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19830612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riba-roja d'Ebre – Riba-roja Reservoir loop from Faió - la Pobla de Massaluca&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 44.6 miles (71.8 km) path with significant ascents.

Q: How long do road cycling routes typically take around Pantà de Riba-roja?
A: Route durations vary significantly based on distance and difficulty. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cinca River – Fraga loop from Mequinenza / Mequinensa&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in under two hours. More challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992605850/fayon-maella-caspe-circular-from-mequinenza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fayón - Maella - Caspe — circular from Mequinenza&lt;/a&gt;, can take over six hours.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Pantà de Riba-roja?
A: The road cycling routes in Pantà de Riba-roja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the scenic views of the Ebro River and reservoir, and the options available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the Pantà de Riba-roja area?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992605850/fayon-maella-caspe-circular-from-mequinenza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fayón - Maella - Caspe — circular from Mequinenza&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150641" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cinca River – Fraga loop from Mequinenza / Mequinensa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling around Pantà de Riba-roja?
A: The region offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for longer rides. While specific winter conditions are not detailed, the varied terrain means you can likely find suitable routes even in cooler months, though some higher elevation sections might be more exposed.

Q: Are there options for family-friendly road biking in Pantà de Riba-roja?
A: While the guide focuses on road cycling, which can be demanding, the presence of easy routes with minimal elevation gain suggests options for families with older children or those looking for a more relaxed ride. Look for routes classified as 'easy' for suitable options.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Pantà de Riba-roja?
A: Many routes start from towns and villages around the reservoir, such as Mequinenza, Faió, or La Pobla de Massaluca. These locations typically offer public parking facilities, making them convenient starting points for your cycling adventures.

Q: Are there any viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many routes offer scenic viewpoints, particularly those that hug the banks of the Ebro River and the reservoir. The surrounding terrain provides elevated positions for panoramic views of the water and the confluence of the Segre, Cinca, and Ebro rivers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Ebro in Mequinenza&lt;/a&gt; also offers notable views.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Pantà de Riba-roja region?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes available in the Pantà de Riba-roja region, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels for cyclists to explore.
tips: Beautiful views of the Río Matarraña from the bridge that separates Aragon and Catalonia. From the beginning of the bridge on the Aragonés side we will see the popular mouth of the Peña Bugarreig railway tunnel (Property of Cultural Interest)

The castle, which is now not preserved in its entirety, was bordered by the old riverbed of the Guadalope river, now part of the Mequinensa reservoir. It is one of the most well-known buildings in the city of Caspe, where it is located at the top of the town itself. This

We are in the Old Town of Mequinenza with views of the Mequinenza Castle, a Gothic castle-palace from the late 14th and early 15th centuries located on a hill at an altitude of 185 meters, being a strategic place that offers a spectacular panoramic view of the confluence from the waters of the Segre, Cinca and Ebro rivers. Currently, the Castle and its surroundings are private property of the Endesa Foundation. (that is why we will not be able to approach with the bicycle)

More information to book guided tours: museosdemequinenza.com/castillo-de-mequinenza

waypoints.name: View of the Matarraña River from the Aragón-Catalonia Bridge
